contribute: New supply chain attacks can be reported via GitHub Issues. Each entry is reviewed and sourced before inclusion.
curation_policy: Two entry types: trojanized packages (specific malicious versions pushed by attackers, matched via malicious_versions) and high-severity exploitable CVEs in widely-installed packages (matched via vulnerable_ranges). Excluded: low-severity DoS, decade-old chaff, deprecated APIs.

threats.name: @angular/ssr
threats.attack_vector: Open redirect and request steering via percent-encoded X-Forwarded-Prefix header. An attacker who controls the upstream proxy or can spoof the header makes the Angular SSR runtime emit absolute redirects to attacker-controlled domains, leaking auth state on follow-redirects.
threats.remediation: Upgrade @angular/ssr to >=19.0.4. Strip X-Forwarded-Prefix at your edge proxy unless explicitly required.

threats.name: @ledgerhq/connect-kit-loader
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer credentials phished via fake pull-request review email; attacker pushed three versions with a wallet-drainer that swapped Wallet-Connect handlers to attacker-controlled drain endpoints. Hundreds of dApps using this loader were affected for ~2 hours before takedown.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin to @ledgerhq/connect-kit-loader@1.1.8 or later.
2. Audit any dApps that integrated this loader during the affected window.
3. Rotate any wallet keys that signed transactions during the window.

threats.name: @solana/web3.js
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer credentials compromised; two trojanized versions exfiltrated private keys and signing material from any Node process that imported the library. Detected within ~5 hours but Solana RPC dApps that updated during the window leaked keys.
threats.remediation:
1. Upgrade to @solana/web3.js@1.95.8 or later.
2. Rotate every keypair that touched the affected machine.
3. Inspect transaction history for unauthorized transfers.

threats.name: @xmldom/xmldom
threats.attack_vector: Continuation of the xmldom node-injection class on the maintained fork. Same family of bugs in DocumentType / ProcessingInstruction / Comment serialization.
threats.remediation: Upgrade @xmldom/xmldom to >= 0.8.5.

threats.name: anchor-bn
threats.attack_vector: Solana ecosystem typosquat: hijacks wallet-connect calls when imported into a dApp; redirects user signatures to an attacker drain address. Caught and removed within hours.
threats.remediation:
1. `npm uninstall anchor-bn`.
2. Audit any wallet-connect code paths that imported it.

threats.name: axios
threats.attack_vector: Trojanized dependency plain-crypto-js@4.2.1 deploys a remote access trojan (RAT) via postinstall script.
threats.remediation: 1. Run: npm uninstall axios && npm install axios@1.14.0. 2. Delete node_modules and reinstall: rm -rf node_modules && npm install. 3. Search for 'plain-crypto-js' in node_modules. If present, your system may be compromised. 4. Rotate any credentials/tokens accessible from the affected machine.

threats.name: babelcli
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of babel-cli. Same author as crossenv; postinstall exfiltrates env vars to the same dropoff endpoint.
threats.remediation: Remove babelcli; replace with babel-cli. Rotate any credentials present in the affected environment.

threats.name: body-parser
threats.attack_vector: DoS via crafted Content-Type header that triggers worst-case URL-encoded parser behavior. Single request hangs the worker; concurrent requests DoS.
threats.remediation: Upgrade body-parser to >=1.20.3.

threats.name: braces
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS via deeply-nested brace expansion patterns. Exploitable wherever braces is reached with untrusted input (often via micromatch).
threats.remediation: Upgrade braces to >=3.0.3.

threats.name: coa
threats.attack_vector: Same attacker chain as ua-parser-js: stolen maintainer credentials pushed seven trojanized versions over two days. Postinstall script downloads a credential stealer + crypto miner; targets Windows specifically.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin coa to 2.0.2 (last known good) and lock the version.
2. Treat any Windows machine that did `npm install` while a bad version was current as compromised; rotate credentials and audit scheduled tasks.

threats.name: colors
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer (marak) intentionally published versions that print 'LIBERTY LIBERTY LIBERTY' followed by a stream of zalgo glyphs in an infinite loop, breaking any app that depended on the package. Not malware in the strict sense, but a deliberate denial-of-service against every downstream user.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin colors to 1.4.0 or migrate to chalk / kleur.
2. The package is now under new stewardship; later versions are clean.

threats.name: cookie
threats.attack_vector: Cookie name/path/domain inputs were not validated, allowing CRLF injection that smuggles arbitrary Set-Cookie or response headers via untrusted input reaching cookie.serialize().
threats.remediation: Upgrade cookie to >=0.7.0.

threats.name: crossenv
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of cross-env. Postinstall script exfiltrates environment variables (frequently AWS credentials, API tokens) to a Heroku endpoint.
threats.remediation: Remove crossenv and replace with cross-env. Audit any environment that ran the postinstall â€” assume secrets exposed.

threats.name: discord-selfbot-v14
threats.attack_vector: Malicious package that grabs the user's Discord token and exfiltrates it via a webhook. Marketed to Discord-bot developers.
threats.remediation: Remove discord-selfbot-v14. Rotate Discord tokens. Use the official discord.js library.

threats.name: eslint-config-eslint
threats.attack_vector: Same eslint-scope attacker chain. Version 5.0.2 included the same npm-token exfiltration postinstall.
threats.remediation: Upgrade eslint-config-eslint to >= 5.0.3 and rotate npm tokens on affected machines.

threats.name: eslint-scope
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer's npm credentials were stolen; attacker published 3.7.2 with a postinstall that read .npmrc and exfiltrated npm tokens to a Pastebin endpoint, enabling further package compromise.
threats.remediation:
1. Upgrade eslint-scope to >= 3.7.3.
2. If the bad version was installed, rotate every npm token on the affected machine â€” tokens may have been exfiltrated.

threats.name: event-stream
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer transferred ownership; new maintainer added flatmap-stream@0.1.1 as a dependency. flatmap-stream contained encrypted bitcoin-wallet-stealer payload that activated only inside the Copay app build chain.
threats.remediation:
1. npm uninstall event-stream and replace with a maintained alternative.
2. If your build also pulled flatmap-stream, audit any wallet keys stored in the affected dev environment.
3. Pin event-stream to <= 3.3.5 if you must keep it.

threats.name: express
threats.attack_vector: Open redirect via `res.location()`: untrusted input is redirected to attacker-controlled destinations because the encoder did not validate scheme.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Express to >=4.20.0. Always validate redirect destinations against an allow-list.

threats.name: express-fileupload
threats.attack_vector: Prototype pollution via parseNested: a crafted multipart upload with `__proto__` keys pollutes Object.prototype, escalating to RCE in many downstream code paths.
threats.remediation: Upgrade express-fileupload to >=1.1.10. Set `parseNested: false` if you can't upgrade.

threats.name: faker
threats.attack_vector: Same maintainer rage event as colors. Version 6.6.6 was published with the entire library replaced by an empty README â€” every downstream consumer broke immediately.
threats.remediation:
1. Migrate to @faker-js/faker (community fork, actively maintained).
2. Pin to the last known good version 5.5.3 if you must keep the original namespace.

threats.name: flatmap-stream
threats.attack_vector: Standalone malicious package that delivered the bitcoin-wallet stealer payload during the event-stream supply chain attack. Contains an encrypted blob that decrypts and runs only when the host app's package.json description matches Copay.
threats.remediation: Remove flatmap-stream entirely. There is no safe version; the package was published by the attacker.

threats.name: flatted
threats.attack_vector: Prototype pollution via parse(). A crafted input string can set Object.prototype keys, enabling escalation in any code path that subsequently iterates a fresh object.
threats.remediation: Upgrade flatted to >= 3.2.7.

threats.name: follow-redirects
threats.attack_vector: Authorization header leakage on cross-origin redirects. The library forwarded credentials when an HTTPS request redirected to a different host, exposing bearer tokens to attacker-controlled servers.
threats.remediation: Upgrade follow-redirects to >= 1.15.6. If on a vulnerable version, rotate any bearer tokens used against external services.

threats.name: getcookies
threats.attack_vector: Backdoor package that intercepted HTTP requests on Express servers via a custom header trigger; allowed remote command execution. Pulled in transitively via http-fetch-cookies and mailparser, infecting tens of thousands of server installs before takedown.
threats.remediation: Remove getcookies and any transitive callers (http-fetch-cookies, older mailparser). There is no safe version.

threats.name: handlebars
threats.attack_vector: Prototype pollution in template compiler enables RCE: a crafted template input mutates Object.prototype and injects a constructor that runs arbitrary JS during render.
threats.remediation:
1. Upgrade handlebars to >=4.7.7.
2. Never pass untrusted input to Handlebars.compile().

threats.name: hono
threats.attack_vector: Two simultaneous 2026 advisories: (1) bodyLimit() bypass for chunked or unknown-Content-Length requests lets an attacker exceed configured limits and DoS the worker; (2) unvalidated JSX tag names allow HTML injection in JSX-rendered responses, enabling stored XSS in any Hono route that takes user input.
threats.remediation: Upgrade hono to >=4.6.10. Validate any user-controlled JSX tag names before rendering.

threats.name: ip
threats.attack_vector: isPrivate() and isPublic() returned wrong answers for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses, allowing SSRF defenses based on this library to be bypassed by encoding internal IPs as ::ffff:127.0.0.1 etc.
threats.remediation: Upgrade ip to >= 2.0.1. Re-test any SSRF guard that relied on isPrivate().

threats.name: jsonpath-plus
threats.attack_vector: Arbitrary remote code execution via unsafe eval() of JSONPath expressions. If user input ever reaches a JSONPath query, an attacker can run arbitrary JavaScript inside the host process.
threats.remediation: Upgrade jsonpath-plus to >= 10.0.0. Treat any service that took user-controlled JSONPath input on a vulnerable version as compromised.

threats.name: jsonwebtoken
threats.attack_vector: Key confusion / signature bypass: when verify() is called without explicit algorithms, an HMAC-signed token can be accepted by an RSA-keyed verifier (or vice versa) by changing the alg header. Reaches RCE in some misconfigured handlers, full auth bypass in most. Three companion CVEs in the same advisory cycle.
threats.remediation:
1. Upgrade jsonwebtoken to >=9.0.0.
2. Always pass an explicit `algorithms` array to verify(). Never call verify() without it.
3. Audit any auth code path that accepts external tokens for the JWT-NO-ALG family of bypasses.

threats.name: kodiak2k
threats.attack_vector: Same campaign as warbeast2000. Postinstall payload exfiltrates Solana / Bitcoin wallet files and SSH keys.
threats.remediation: Same as warbeast2000.

threats.name: lodash
threats.attack_vector: Prototype pollution in zipObjectDeep / mergeWith / set. A crafted input can set Object.prototype keys, enabling privilege escalation in code paths that iterate fresh objects without hasOwnProperty checks.
threats.remediation: Upgrade lodash to >= 4.17.21.

threats.name: micromatch
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS via crafted glob pattern. User-supplied input that reaches micromatch() (file-name filters, route matchers) hangs the worker. Widely depended on.
threats.remediation: Upgrade micromatch to >=4.0.8.

threats.name: minimatch
threats.attack_vector: Path-traversal via crafted glob patterns; widely depended-upon (npm, node-glob, mocha). Exploitable by any tool that accepts glob input from untrusted sources.
threats.remediation: Upgrade minimatch to >= 3.0.5.

threats.name: nanoid
threats.attack_vector: Predictable IDs when alphabet is shorter than size argument; non-uniform distribution lets attackers guess upcoming IDs above uniform odds. Exploitable for session-token / capability-URL prediction.
threats.remediation: Upgrade nanoid to >=3.3.8 (v3) or >=5.0.9 (v5).

threats.name: next
threats.attack_vector: Cache poisoning in Pages Router: a crafted request with specific headers can cause a response intended for one user to be served to others from the cache.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Next.js to >=14.2.10 (14.x branch) or >=15.0.4 (15.x branch).

threats.name: nitro
threats.attack_vector: Two simultaneous 2026 advisories in Nitro (Nuxt's server engine): (1) open redirect via protocol-relative URL bypass in wildcard route rules, exploitable on any Nuxt app with wildcard routes; (2) proxy scope bypass via percent-encoded path traversal in routeRules, letting requests reach internal services that should be off-limits.
threats.remediation: Upgrade nitro to >=2.10.0; transitively upgrade Nuxt to a version pinned at the patched nitro.

threats.name: noblox.js-proxy
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of noblox.js. Postinstall downloads a Discord token grabber; the package was used to compromise gaming-bot dev environments.
threats.remediation: Remove noblox.js-proxy. If installed, rotate Discord tokens and audit Discord account for unauthorized actions.

threats.name: node-fetch
threats.attack_vector: Cross-origin redirect leak: cookies and Authorization headers were forwarded to redirect targets on different hosts, exposing session tokens to attacker-controlled servers.
threats.remediation: Upgrade node-fetch to >=2.6.7 / >=3.1.1.

threats.name: node-forge
threats.attack_vector: ASN.1 validator desynchronization lets an attacker craft an X.509 certificate that the verifier accepts but the consumer interprets differently. Exploitable in any pipeline that uses node-forge to validate certificates from untrusted sources.
threats.remediation: Upgrade node-forge to >= 1.3.0.

threats.name: node-ipc
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer added geo-targeted file-deletion code as a protest against the Russia-Ukraine war ('peacenotwar'). On systems with IP addresses geolocating to Russia or Belarus, the package overwrites files with a heart-emoji byte pattern. Hit thousands of unrelated downstream apps including the US Department of Defense's Vue.js stack.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin node-ipc to 9.2.1.
2. If running on infrastructure in Russia or Belarus, audit recent file activity and restore from backup.

threats.name: node-tar
threats.attack_vector: Path traversal via crafted tar entries. A malicious archive can write files outside the extraction directory by abusing hardlink + symlink handling and Unicode normalization on case-insensitive filesystems (macOS APFS).
threats.remediation: Upgrade node-tar to >= 6.2.1. Audit any service that extracts tar archives from untrusted sources.

threats.name: nth-check
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS in CSS pseudo-class :nth-check parser. Reachable through any HTML/CSS pipeline that processes untrusted input (SVG rendering, sanitization).
threats.remediation: Upgrade nth-check to >=2.0.1. Frequently reached through react-scripts; upgrade your build toolchain.

threats.name: path-to-regexp
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS via crafted route patterns. Affects Express by transitive dependency. A single request with a maliciously crafted path hangs the worker; concurrent requests trivially DoS.
threats.remediation: Upgrade path-to-regexp to 0.1.10, 1.9.0, 3.3.0, 6.3.0, or 8.0.0+.

threats.name: peacenotwar
threats.attack_vector: Standalone payload package referenced by the trojanized node-ipc versions. Drops a WITH-LOVE-FROM-AMERICA.txt manifesto on every desktop and writes geo-targeted destructive payloads.
threats.remediation: Remove peacenotwar; there is no safe version. It is solely the attacker's payload package.

threats.name: postcss
threats.attack_vector: Parser confusion: a crafted CSS input with `\r` line endings is mis-parsed, causing the parser to hang or yield wrong AST. Exploitable in any build pipeline that processes user-uploaded CSS.
threats.remediation: Upgrade postcss to >=8.4.31.

threats.name: rc
threats.attack_vector: Same attacker as ua-parser-js / coa. Three impostor versions were published with a postinstall payload identical to the coa attack: download Cobalt Strike loader and password stealer.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin rc to 1.2.8 / 1.3.8 / 2.3.8 (last known good).
2. Rebuild the affected dev environment from a clean OS install if `npm install` ran on a Windows machine while a bad version was published.

threats.name: react-router
threats.attack_vector: Server-side rendering (SSR) cross-site scripting via the ScrollRestoration component. Reflected user input can break out of the JSON serialization and inject arbitrary HTML on the rendered page.
threats.remediation: Upgrade react-router to >= 6.30.2 (or >= 7.6.4 on the v7 line).

threats.name: rollup
threats.attack_vector: Path-traversal vulnerability in the file-resolution layer allowed a malicious dependency to write files outside the build output directory during a build. Exploitable by any project that builds packages it does not fully control.
threats.remediation: Upgrade rollup to >= 4.22.4.

threats.name: rspack-cli
threats.attack_vector: Compromised maintainer credentials pushed two versions with a postinstall data-exfiltration script targeting environment variables and SSH keys. Caught quickly; <700 installs reported.
threats.remediation:
1. Upgrade to rspack-cli@1.1.9 or later.
2. Rotate environment-variable secrets exposed to the affected machine.

threats.name: semver
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS in `new Range()`. A crafted range string triggers catastrophic backtracking. Widely depended on; exploitable any time a service accepts user-supplied semver ranges.
threats.remediation: Upgrade to semver@7.5.2 / 6.3.1 / 5.7.2 or later.

threats.name: send
threats.attack_vector: Reflected XSS in error responses: when an invalid path is passed to send/serve-static, the path is reflected into the 404 page without escaping.
threats.remediation: Upgrade send to >=0.19.0; transitively, upgrade Express to >=4.20.0 or >=5.0.0.

threats.name: serve-static
threats.attack_vector: Same reflected-XSS class as send (its dependency): error responses reflected unescaped path components.
threats.remediation: Upgrade serve-static to >=1.16.0.

threats.name: socket.io
threats.attack_vector: DoS via crafted Engine.IO header. A malformed `EIO` parameter triggers an unhandled exception in the handshake path, crashing the worker. Exploitable on any service that exposes a Socket.IO endpoint to untrusted clients.
threats.remediation: Upgrade socket.io to >=2.5.1 (v2 branch) or >=4.6.2 (v3+ branches).

threats.name: tar
threats.attack_vector: Same hardlink path-traversal class as node-tar; both packages share extraction code paths in older releases.
threats.remediation: Upgrade tar to >= 6.2.1.

threats.name: ua-parser-js
threats.attack_vector: Maintainer's npm credentials were stolen and three malicious versions were published. Each runs a postinstall script that downloads and executes a Linux/macOS crypto-miner plus a Windows password stealer that exfiltrates browser-stored credentials.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin ua-parser-js to 0.7.30 / 0.8.1 / 1.0.1 or later.
2. Audit any machine that ran `npm install` while a bad version was current â€” assume browser-stored credentials are compromised.
3. Rotate passwords saved in Chrome / Edge / Firefox on affected machines.

threats.name: vm2
threats.attack_vector: Sandbox escape via Promise handler. An attacker who can submit JavaScript to a vm2 sandbox can execute arbitrary code on the host process. Library is now deprecated; the maintainer recommends migrating to isolated-vm. 2026 wave: five new sandbox-escape CVEs disclosed in early 2026 (CVE-2026-43997, 43999, 44001, 44005, 44006) reinforce that vm2 is unsalvageable for hardening; all production users should migrate to isolated-vm immediately.
threats.remediation: Migrate away from vm2; use isolated-vm or Node's vm.SourceTextModule with a ContextifyContext. vm2 is end-of-life.

threats.name: warbeast2000
threats.attack_vector: Crypto wallet stealer typosquatting Web3 dev tooling. On install runs a postinstall script that reads ~/.config/Solana, ~/.bitcoin, browser wallet extensions, and ~/.ssh/* and exfiltrates to an attacker server.
threats.remediation:
1. `npm uninstall warbeast2000`.
2. Treat machine as compromised: rotate every wallet, SSH key, cloud credential.
3. Audit wallet transaction logs.

threats.name: ws
threats.attack_vector: DoS / request-smuggling via crafted HTTP headers in the WebSocket upgrade handshake. Exploitable on any service that accepts WebSocket connections from untrusted origins.
threats.remediation: Upgrade ws to >= 8.17.1 (or matching patch on 7.x / 6.x / 5.x).

threats.name: xmldom
threats.attack_vector: XML node injection through unvalidated comment / processing-instruction / DocumentType serialization. Three distinct CVEs share the same root cause.
threats.remediation: Migrate to @xmldom/xmldom >= 0.7.0 (the original xmldom package is no longer maintained).

threats.name: aiohttp
threats.attack_vector: HTTP request smuggling via mishandled Content-Length parsing. An attacker can desynchronize a frontend reverse proxy from the aiohttp backend, smuggling poisoned requests onto another user's session.
threats.remediation: Upgrade aiohttp to >=3.9.2.

threats.name: black
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS in `lines2` tokenizer. A crafted Python source file with long sequences of specific tokens hangs the formatter for minutes, blocking any CI pipeline that runs Black on user-submitted code.
threats.remediation: Upgrade black to >=24.3.0.

threats.name: blender-bpy
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat targeting Blender Python script developers (legitimate `bpy` ships only with Blender). Downloads BlackCap stealer + W4SP modules on import.
threats.remediation:
1. `pip uninstall blender-bpy`.
2. Treat host as compromised.

threats.name: colourama
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of colorama (British spelling). Replaces every clipboard cryptocurrency address with the attacker's address while the package is imported, redirecting transfers.
threats.remediation: Remove colourama; install the legitimate `colorama` package. Audit recent crypto-currency transactions on the affected machine for redirected payments.

threats.name: cryptography
threats.attack_vector: NULL pointer dereference in PKCS#12 parsing led to denial-of-service. More serious in the same line: a memory-corruption fix for PKCS7 signatures shipped in 42.0.4.
threats.remediation: Upgrade cryptography to >= 42.0.4.

threats.name: ctx
threats.attack_vector: Trojanized takeover of an abandoned package. New maintainer added code that exfiltrates os.environ (frequently AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, DB credentials) to a Heroku endpoint at every import.
threats.remediation:
1. Pin ctx to 0.1.2 (last known good).
2. Rotate every credential that was in environment variables on the affected machine â€” assume AWS keys, DB passwords, API tokens are exposed.
3. Audit AWS CloudTrail / IAM access logs for unusual activity from new IPs.

threats.name: diffusers
threats.attack_vector: Untrusted-code execution via a None.py bypass mechanism in the model-loading pipeline. A model on Hugging Face Hub with a crafted file can execute arbitrary Python on `from_pretrained()` even when `safetensors=True` is intended.
threats.remediation: Upgrade diffusers to >=0.30.4. Always pin model sources to verified Hub organizations; never load from untrusted accounts.

threats.name: django
threats.attack_vector: URLValidator regex catastrophic backtracking allowing DoS, plus a memory-leak in HttpResponse on slow uploads. Fixed in the listed point releases.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Django to the matching patch release on your major line.

threats.name: easyfuncs
threats.attack_vector: W4SP Stealer wave. Masquerades as a utility-functions library; on import runs the W4SP stealer.
threats.remediation: Same recovery as request3.

threats.name: fastapi
threats.attack_vector: ReDoS via crafted Content-Type header. Reachable on any FastAPI app that processes form data without an upstream length limit.
threats.remediation: Upgrade FastAPI to >=0.109.1.

threats.name: flask-cors
threats.attack_vector: Case-sensitive origin matching flaw: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header validation compared against the configured allow-list with case sensitivity, while browsers normalize the request Origin to lowercase. An attacker controlling an origin like `EXAMPLE.com` (uppercase) could bypass policies set for `example.com`, enabling cross-origin reads against authenticated APIs.
threats.remediation: Upgrade flask-cors to >=4.0.1. Ensure your CORS allow-list compares origins case-insensitively if you can't upgrade immediately.

threats.name: gradio
threats.attack_vector: Local file disclosure via path traversal in the file-component download endpoint. An unauthenticated attacker reading a public Gradio demo can fetch arbitrary files from the host filesystem.
threats.remediation: Upgrade gradio to >=4.19.2. Don't expose Gradio demos to untrusted networks until upgraded.

threats.name: jeIlyfish
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of jellyfish (capital I instead of l). Reads the user's SSH and GPG keys plus crypto wallet files and exfiltrates them to a remote server during import.
threats.remediation: Remove jeIlyfish; install the legitimate `jellyfish` package. Rotate SSH and GPG keys on affected machines.

threats.name: jupyter-server
threats.attack_vector: Authenticated XSS in the file-listing UI. A user with write access can upload a file with a crafted name that executes JavaScript in another user's session.
threats.remediation: Upgrade jupyter-server to >=2.14.1.

threats.name: keras
threats.attack_vector: DoS via malicious model files causing excessive memory allocation. A crafted .keras file consumed by Keras's model-loading path triggers a multi-gigabyte allocation and crashes the worker. Exploitable in any service that accepts user-uploaded models.
threats.remediation: Upgrade keras to >=3.7.0. Set memory limits on worker pools; gate model uploads to known users.

threats.name: litellm
threats.attack_vector: Credential harvester exfiltrates environment variables and API keys. Installs persistent .pth backdoor in site-packages that survives pip uninstall.
threats.remediation: 1. Run: pip uninstall litellm && pip install litellm==1.82.6. 2. Check site-packages for suspicious .pth files: python -c "import site; print(site.getsitepackages())". Look for .pth files you don't recognize. The backdoor survives uninstall. 3. Rotate ALL API keys, tokens, and credentials from environment variables. 4. Check ~/.local/lib and conda envs for the same .pth artifacts.

threats.name: lxml
threats.attack_vector: XSS via `Cleaner.clean_html()` not stripping `<style>`-injected JavaScript URIs. Apps relying on Cleaner to sanitize untrusted HTML are exposed.
threats.remediation: Upgrade lxml to >=4.9.1. Don't rely on Cleaner.clean_html() as your sole sanitizer.

threats.name: misp-modules
threats.attack_vector: Two simultaneous 2026 advisories in MISP-modules (the threat-intelligence platform's expansion module surface): (1) Missing CSRF protection on the homepage blueprint allows any authenticated user to perform privileged actions cross-site; (2) Unsafe remote-resource fetching in expansion modules without origin validation enables SSRF.
threats.remediation: Upgrade misp-modules to >=2.4.198. If your MISP serves untrusted SOC users, gate write operations behind extra approval in the meantime.

threats.name: noblesse
threats.attack_vector: Discord token grabber and credit-card sniffer. Targeted Windows-based bot developers.
threats.remediation: Remove noblesse; rotate Discord tokens. Audit credit cards entered on affected machines.

threats.name: phpass
threats.attack_vector: Same attacker chain as ctx, same env-var exfiltration payload.
threats.remediation: Remove phpass; rotate environment-variable secrets on affected machines.

threats.name: pillow
threats.attack_vector: Buffer overflow in PIL.ImageMath.eval allowed remote code execution when processing images from untrusted sources via crafted format strings.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Pillow to >= 10.3.0.

threats.name: praisonai
threats.attack_vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution via tool override configuration bypass. An attacker who can submit a tool definition to a PraisonAI agent endpoint can override the registered tool handler and execute arbitrary Python in the agent process. Exploitable on any PraisonAI deployment that accepts tool definitions from untrusted users.
threats.remediation: Upgrade praisonai to >=2.0.6. Strict-validate every tool definition before registration; gate tool registration to admin users only.

threats.name: praisonaiagents
threats.attack_vector: SSRF protection bypass mechanism. The library's tool execution layer accepts URLs without re-validating them against the SSRF allow-list, letting an LLM tool call reach internal services. Companion advisory to praisonai's RCE.
threats.remediation: Upgrade praisonaiagents to >=2.0.6. Treat any URL reaching tool execution as untrusted; re-validate against your SSRF allow-list.

threats.name: pymafka
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of `pykafka`. On import downloads and runs a beacon to an attacker C2 server; reports host external IP, OS, home dir contents. Caught in 2 days; ~325 downloads before takedown.
threats.remediation:
1. `pip uninstall pymafka`.
2. Inspect host for unexpected outbound traffic during install window.
3. Rotate any credentials accessible from the host.

threats.name: pyrequest
threats.attack_vector: W4SP Stealer wave. Typosquat of `requests`. Same payload as request3 / urlllib3.
threats.remediation: Same recovery as request3.

threats.name: python3-dateutil
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of python-dateutil. Same payload as jeIlyfish (same author): exfiltrates SSH keys and crypto wallet files.
threats.remediation: Remove python3-dateutil; install the legitimate `python-dateutil` package. Rotate SSH and GPG keys on affected machines.

threats.name: pytorch-lightning
threats.attack_vector: PyPI maintainer-token compromise on 2026-04-30. Attacker pushed tampered builds 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 directly to PyPI (GitHub source repo was not touched); both versions live for ~42 minutes before quarantine. Payload: hidden _runtime/ directory containing a Bun-based JavaScript credential stealer that runs automatically on `import lightning` or `import pytorch_lightning`, exfiltrating environment variables, .env files, GitHub tokens, AWS/Azure/GCP credentials, browser-stored creds (Chrome, Firefox, Brave), and crypto wallets. Notable: plants persistence hooks targeting Claude Code and VS Code, the first documented in-the-wild abuse of Claude Code's hook system. Family: Mini Shai-Hulud, same author lineage as the npm Shai-Hulud worm wave. Last clean version: 2.6.1 (2026-01-30). Advisory: GHSA-w37p-236h-pfx3.
persistence_artifacts: _runtime/ directory inside the installed package (contains the Bun-based JS stealer)
Claude Code hook entries planted under ~/.claude/
threats.remediation:
1. Pin pytorch-lightning to <=2.6.1 (or to a clean release published after the 2.6.3 quarantine).
2. Wipe pip cache and reinstall: `pip cache purge && pip install --force-reinstall 'pytorch-lightning<=2.6.1'`.
3. If you installed or upgraded pytorch-lightning or lightning during the disclosure window on 2026-04-30, audit ~/.claude/ for unfamiliar hooks, audit installed VS Code extensions, rotate GitHub and cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP) tokens, and check for unauthorized outbound traffic from any host that imported the module.

threats.name: pyyaml
threats.attack_vector: yaml.load() with the default Loader executed arbitrary Python on untrusted input. Even after the migration to safe loaders, pinned old versions remain widely deployed.
threats.remediation: Upgrade PyYAML to >= 5.4 and migrate yaml.load(...) calls to yaml.safe_load(...) at every callsite.

threats.name: request
threats.attack_vector: Typosquat of `requests`. Performs HTTP exfiltration of os.environ and ~/.aws/credentials at every import.
threats.remediation: Remove `request`; install the legitimate `requests` package. Rotate AWS credentials and any secrets present in os.environ on affected machines.

threats.name: request3
threats.attack_vector: W4SP Stealer wave. Typosquat of `requests`. On import downloads and runs the W4SP stealer payload that exfiltrates browser-stored passwords, Discord tokens, and crypto-wallet files via Discord webhook.
threats.remediation:
1. `pip uninstall request3`.
2. Treat host as compromised: rotate browser-saved passwords, Discord tokens, every reachable credential.
3. Inspect Discord audit logs for unauthorized webhook activity.

threats.name: requests
threats.attack_vector: Session.get() / Session.post() forwarded the Proxy-Authorization header on cross-origin redirects, leaking corporate-proxy credentials to redirect targets.
threats.remediation: Upgrade requests to >= 2.32.0.

threats.name: scrapy
threats.attack_vector: XML External Entity injection via the XmlItemExporter when crawling targets that return XML. A crafted XML response can read arbitrary files on the scrapy host.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Scrapy to >=2.11.2.

threats.name: secretslib
threats.attack_vector: Trojan masquerading as a secrets-management library. On import, downloads a Linux/macOS Monero miner into a hidden subdirectory and adds it to crontab for persistence.
threats.remediation:
1. `pip uninstall secretslib`.
2. Check `crontab -l` for entries pointing to .secrets-helper and remove.
3. Remove ~/.cache/.secrets-helper/.
4. Audit for unauthorized outbound traffic to mining pools.

threats.name: starlette
threats.attack_vector: Multipart parser DoS: a crafted multipart/form-data upload with many small parts exhausts CPU. Affects FastAPI by transit.
threats.remediation: Upgrade starlette to >=0.40.0; transitively upgrade FastAPI to >=0.115.0.

threats.name: torch
threats.attack_vector: Arbitrary code execution via `torch.load()` of an untrusted file. The pickle deserializer is unsafe; loading a model from an untrusted source executes attacker code.
threats.remediation: Upgrade torch to >=2.2.0. Pass `weights_only=True` to `torch.load()` for untrusted files.

threats.name: torchtriton
threats.attack_vector: Dependency-confusion attack against pytorch-nightly. PyPI's torchtriton (malicious) was preferred over the internal index version with the same name. The package collected /etc/hosts, /etc/passwd, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh/* and uploaded to an attacker server during import.
threats.remediation:
1. If you installed pytorch-nightly between 2022-12-25 and 2022-12-30, treat the machine as compromised: rotate SSH keys, audit ~/.ssh/known_hosts for new entries, and assume /etc/hosts contents leaked.
2. Use the official triton package; never install torchtriton from PyPI.

threats.name: transformers
threats.attack_vector: Arbitrary code execution via untrusted pickle deserialization. A model uploaded to Hugging Face Hub with a crafted pytorch_model.bin can execute arbitrary Python on load.
threats.remediation: Upgrade transformers to >=4.38.0. Use `safetensors` format for untrusted models.

threats.name: urllib3
threats.attack_vector: Cookie / proxy-auth credential leakage on cross-origin redirects. Sensitive headers were forwarded when an HTTPS request redirected to a different host.
threats.remediation: Upgrade urllib3 to >= 1.26.19 or >= 2.2.2. Rotate any bearer tokens that may have been forwarded during cross-origin redirects.

threats.name: urlllib3
threats.attack_vector: W4SP Stealer wave. Typosquat of `urllib3` (extra `l`). Same payload as request3.
threats.remediation:
1. `pip uninstall urlllib3` (note extra `l`).
2. Same recovery as request3.

threats.name: weblate
threats.attack_vector: Two simultaneous 2026 advisories: (1) Stored XSS via maliciously-crafted Markdown content in translation comments and project descriptions; (2) Enumeration of private translations through an unprotected screenshot API endpoint. Both exploitable on shared multi-org Weblate deployments.
threats.remediation: Upgrade Weblate to >=5.8.4.

threats.name: werkzeug
threats.attack_vector: Multipart bomb DoS: a crafted upload with tens of thousands of small parts causes memory blowup. Affects Flask by transit.
threats.remediation: Upgrade werkzeug to >=3.0.6.
